text.title: Ruff for Python and Biome for JS/TS as the Sole Linter+Formatter Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: New Python projects MUST use ruff as the SOLE linter + formatter; black / flake8 / isort / pyupgrade / autoflake / pydocstyle are removed.
rule.rationale: ruff covers > 900 rules + format and runs 10-100x faster; multi-tool chain is legacy churn.

rule.statement: Where prettier+eslint is over-engineered (new project, no entrenched eslint plugins), biome is the SOLE formatter + linter for JS/TS.
rule.rationale: biome covers format + lint + import sort with one binary; prettier+eslint setup churn is real.

rule.statement: ruff config lives in pyproject.toml [tool.ruff]; biome config in biome.json. No .ruff.toml / external configs.
rule.rationale: Single source per tool prevents config drift across files.

rule.statement: ruff rule selection MUST be explicit (no `select = ['ALL']` blindly); document why each group is on.
rule.rationale: Implicit ALL pulls in noisy rules that mask important findings.

rule.statement: Both tools MUST run as pre-commit hooks AND in CI.
rule.rationale: Hook = fast feedback; CI = merge gate.

rule.statement: If legacy eslint with heavy custom rules OR external mandate pins different tooling OR project depends on plugins not in ruff/biome, STOP.
rule.rationale: Migration churn on legacy projects > value.
